questions.question: Are D1 and D3 multi-client both implemented as labelled-only (no assertion that fails when divergent)?
questions.how_to_verify: Read the two new `it` blocks. Both use `fc.statistics(...)` and conclude with `expect(true).toBe(true)`. Neither contains `expect(mapsEqual(...)).toBe(true)` or any assertion that would fail on divergence. Running FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS=10000 produces 100% divergent/client1-loses-newer labels without any test failure.
questions.expected_answer: Yes — both tests use fc.statistics to classify outcomes and only assert `expect(true).toBe(true)` at the end.

questions.question: Does each test use fc.statistics to record the divergence distribution?
questions.how_to_verify: Run `npx vitest run src/store/multi-client.property.test.ts --reporter=verbose`. CI log output should show `divergent..100.00%` for D1 and `client1-loses-newer..100.00%` for D3.
questions.expected_answer: Yes — D1 labels 'divergent' vs 'equal'; D3 labels 'client1-loses-newer' vs 'clients-converge'.

questions.question: Test count is 77+N where N >= 2?
questions.how_to_verify: Run `npx vitest run` and check `Tests: N passed`. Baseline was 77; S5 adds 2 tests (D1 + D3); expected total is 79.

questions.question: Naming and comment conventions consistent with AC-X-NAMING-1 and AC-X-NAMING-2?
questions.how_to_verify: Grep the two new `it` blocks: titles start with '[D1]' and '[D3]' respectively (AC-X-NAMING-2). First line inside each body is a comment of the form `// D1: ...` or `// D3: ...` (AC-X-NAMING-1).
questions.expected_answer: Yes — `it('[D1] equal-timestamp two-client updates may diverge — labelled', ...)` with `// D1: two-client concurrent equal-timestamp updates to the same task.` as first body line; same pattern for D3.

questions.question: At FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS=10000, do D1/D3 still not fail (only labels recorded)?
questions.how_to_verify: Run `FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS=10000 npx vitest run src/store/multi-client.property.test.ts`. All 10 tests must pass with no failures. Labels will show divergent/client1-loses-newer at 100% but no counterexample printed.
questions.expected_answer: Yes — all 10 tests pass with 10000 runs. D1/D3 do not fail regardless of run count.

questions.question: No production code changed?
questions.how_to_verify: Run `git diff --stat`. Only `src/store/multi-client.property.test.ts` and files under `specs/` should appear. No changes to `src/store/task-reducer.ts`, `src/store/task-events.ts`, `src/store/task-store.tsx`, `src/marmot/`, or `src/components/`.
questions.expected_answer: Only src/store/multi-client.property.test.ts (test extension) and specs/epic-property-based-invariants/S5-multi-client-labelled-properties/ (story artifacts) are modified.
